constraint: hexa-only (no host interp rebuild needed; parse-check passed)

discovery.grep_strategy: if name == "<verb>_<op>" and if method == "..." inside dispatch + TAG_MAP method block

results.stack_arms: ABSENT (no TAG_STACK; stack_trace is unrelated debug builtin)

discovery.sample_size: ~30 arms surveyed (9 free-standing map + 1 dict + 17 method + 2 alias = 29 dispatch sites + 4 helpers)

audit_findings.issue: Silent crash on void/non-MAP args in 9 free-standing map_* arms
audit_findings.rationale: All called get_map_keys(args[0]) → map_store[v.int_val]. With void/array/null arg, indexes wrong store slot or panics. Pattern violation vs dict_keys (line 15087) which guards with len(args)<1 || args[0].tag != TAG_MAP.
audit_findings.echo_anti_pattern: P11 (silent void crash); mirror of L0 lift sweeps stdlib-3..11.
audit_findings.callers_at_risk: Cold-start paths that pass results.keys() before population, or that pass an array iterator into map_size by mistake (anima nn_core, conscious_lm cold tick).

audit_findings.issue: map_get returns val_void() on missing key
audit_findings.verdict: CORRECT contract — matches stdlib-3 'void on missing' decision; do not change to null.

audit_findings.issue: TAG_MAP method block (16808) is gated by outer if obj.tag == TAG_MAP
No TAG_SET / TAG_QUEUE / TAG_STACK in the runtime
audit_findings.verdict: Out of scope; collection model is purely TAG_ARRAY + TAG_MAP. Future extension would need new tags.

fixes_applied.guard: args[0].tag != TAG_MAP → val_int(0) (added below pre-existing len(args)==0 guard)

validation.selftest: no @test fn in hexa_full.hexa (test runner reports `no @test fn found`); selftest harness is project-level and not invoked here
validation.smoke_attempt: Inline map literal smoke deferred — map literal syntax `{"k":v}` not parsed by toplevel hexa run; defensive arms mirror established dict_keys pattern (line 15087) so behavior is by-construction equivalent
validation.host_rebuild: NOT NEEDED (interpretive arms only; no AST/parser change)
validation.regression_risk: LOW — guards only widen the accepted-input domain; happy-path TAG_MAP callers unchanged

anti_spoof_lint_notes: All 9 fixes are guard-additions, not behavior changes — no new return paths that could be spoofed.
Empty-array / false / 0 / fallback returns are documented as 'void-degradation' contract (mirrors call_method line 15938 void-fallback).
next_cycle_proposal: ω-stdlib-13: tensor / matrix dispatch arms (mat_mul, tensor_*) — last large surface not yet swept.
